title: DB2 Error Messages
text: DB2 Error Messages When an error has occurred, $dberror will be set to the error code DB2 has returned for that particular error. The DB2-generated error text will be available in the message frame, or in the application’s log file when $PUTMESS_LOG_FILE is used in the assignment file. You can look up the error code in the documentation of DB2. The errors returned by DB2 (via $dberror ) are shown in the table: Table 1. $dberror driver error messages Error Message -24 The connector can fetch by primary key only. -25 No more cursors could be allocated. -26 No more statements could be allocated. -27 Connector deleted/updated more or less than 1 row. -28 An illegal request was received by the connector. -29 Already logged on to a database. -30 The connector does not support this version of DB2. -31 The connector does not recognize this DBMS. -32 Could not obtain sufficient dynamic memory. -33 The result row bigger than uiobuf. -34 General logic error in connector. -35 Error in USYS$DB2_PARAMS string. -36 Requested action results in empty fieldlist. -37 Cannot execute next step. Cursor has been released. iSeries On iSeries, you also have the possibility to use the CL command DSPMSGD to look up the error in the QSQLMSG message file. The message identifier to look up is SQL xxxx , where xxxx is the four-digit error number. For example, if the error code is -913 , the identifier is SQL0913 . Related Links $PUTMESS_LOG_FILE Copy the message frame contents to one or more files. $dberror Return the error code reported by the DBMS. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
